title: erikslund pool configuration
description: Configuration for the erikslund solo Bitcoin mining pool (C++ and Python implementations). Unknown keys are rejected.

$schema.description: Editor hint; ignored by the pool.

bitcoin_nodes.description: bitcoind RPC endpoints, tried in order for failover. The first entry is the primary node.

items.description: A bitcoind RPC endpoint. Unrecognized keys are ignored.

address.description: host:port (or full URL) of the bitcoind RPC interface, e.g. bitcoind:8332.

stratum_listen.description: Stratum bind address(es), host:port. Default binds all interfaces on :3333. Multiple entries bind multiple ports.

api_listen.description: host:port for the read-only HTTP status/metrics API (always enabled). Default 127.0.0.1:7777 (loopback). The API is UNAUTHENTICATED, so expose it deliberately: set 0.0.0.0 (or a specific interface) only where a host port-mapping, private network, or reverse proxy confines it. The bridge/HA deploy stacks override to 0.0.0.0 (loopback-confined); the host-network stacks keep this loopback default.

proxy_protocol_from.description: Trusted upstreams (exact IP or IPv4 CIDR, e.g. "10.0.0.5" or "172.19.0.0/16") allowed to send a PROXY-protocol v1/v2 header on stratum connections; the pool then logs the real client IP. Empty/absent = disabled (all connections direct). Connections from any non-listed source are always treated as direct, so direct (proxy-bypassing) connections still work and a header can't be spoofed.

zmq_block_endpoint.description: bitcoind ZMQ hashblock endpoint, e.g. tcp://127.0.0.1:28332. Requires bitcoind -zmqpubhashblock.

fast_block_notify.description: On a ZMQ block notification, broadcast empty 'stop work' built from the new block hash before the slower getblocktemplate. Default true.

block_poll_milliseconds.description: New-block poll interval in milliseconds (a backup for ZMQ notifications). Default 1000.

work_rebroadcast_seconds.description: How often fresh work is pushed to connected miners, in seconds. Default 30.

initial_difficulty.description: Starting share difficulty for new clients. Default 1.

minimum_difficulty.description: Lowest difficulty variable_difficulty will drop a client to. Default 0.001.

maximum_difficulty.description: Highest difficulty variable_difficulty will raise a client to (0 = no maximum). Default 0.

vardiff_target_shares_per_minute.description: Target shares per minute that variable_difficulty aims each client at. Default 12.

vardiff_retarget_seconds.description: How often variable_difficulty re-evaluates a client, in seconds. Default 60.

extranonce1_size.description: extranonce1 byte length. Default 4. Min 4: a smaller assignment space could wrap and hand two live miners identical work.

version_rolling_mask.description: BIP320 version bits a client may roll, as a hex string (e.g. "1fffe000") or an integer. Default 1fffe000.

coinbase_signature.description: Signature placed in the coinbase of solved blocks.

donation_percent.description: Percentage of the block reward paid to donation_address. Default 0 (disabled).

donation_address.description: Address that receives donation_percent of the block reward.

max_workers_per_address.description: Maximum worker rows itemized individually in one address's stats (users/<address>). Connections beyond this still mine and still count toward the address totals, but their per-worker stats fold into a single shared bucket shown under the bare address -- so one address can't fill its stats file with thousands of worker rows. Not a connection cap. 0 = unlimited. Default 256.

max_clients.description: Upper limit on accepted client connections. Default 1024.

drop_idle_seconds.description: Disconnect clients idle for this many seconds (0 = never). Default 3600.

auth_timeout_seconds.description: Disconnect clients that don't authorize within this many seconds (0 = never). Default 30.

max_protocol_errors.description: Disconnect a client after this many malformed / out-of-order protocol errors since its last accepted share (0 = never). Default 100.

max_line_bytes.description: Maximum length of one Stratum protocol line, in bytes (anti-OOM). Default 16384.

stats_directory.description: Directory for pool/client stats files (pool.status, users/). Default 'stats'.

status_interval_seconds.description: How often the pool.status and users/ files are rewritten, in seconds. Default 30.

user_stats_retention_days.description: Remove a users/<address> stats file once the address has been inactive (disconnected; its file no longer rewritten) for this many days. Connected miners are never pruned. 0 keeps files forever (so does anything above the 36500 cap, but use 0). Default 90.

worker_threads.description: Connection-serving worker threads (C++ epoll reactors; Python event loops on a free-threaded build). 0 = auto: one per usable CPU, clamped to a sane max. Set to your container's CPU limit to avoid over-threading. Ignored on a GIL Python build (always one loop).
